title: How much did Anime Shinji know
display_title: How much did Anime Shinji know
wikitext:
[]: How much did (Anime) Shinji know?             Quote Post by NIkkolas » Wed Aug 06, 2014 12:20 pm So obviously we the audience know all about the giant conspiracy with SEELE and Second and Third Impact and all that. But I only watched the anime through once (and didn't much care for it...) and to see how it compares and contrasts with the anime I am currently reading the manga. I'm at the end of Volume 5 and Kaji just dropped an exposition bomb on Shinji and us. He laid it all out there for him in refreshingly clear langauge - that his pops knew about Second Impact, that Yui was the one who "created" Gendo and the Evas, that the Dead Sea Scrolls tell all about the entire history of human existence, that Angels attack Nerv HQ for a specific reason and that the one behind Gendo, Yui and the rest of it are SEELE. Was Anime Shinji ever this clued into the inner-workings of his universe? Did he ever even know the term SEELE? I don't think so but I honestly can't recall. Might I just say though that Sadamoto's storytelling is a lot more...uh, digestible? He explains it pretty well in one of his interviews in the manga. The manga "bombards" (his word, at least his word in the Viz translation) you with much less needlessly convoluted information than the anime did so it's easier to put all the pieces together. I'm certainly enjoying the manga a lot.

[]:             Quote Post by UrsusArctos » Wed Aug 06, 2014 12:41 pm I don't think Anime-Shinji or any of the pilots for that matter ever uttered "Seele". He was pretty oblivious to most of the things going on in the anime, although he did manage to connect Rei to his mother, and to Gendo's machinations. In EoE, Misato told him all that Kaji had revealed to her, although he was in no state to absorb much of it. How much did he know about what was going on by the bitter end? I'd say, not very much.

[]:             Quote Post by Nuclear Lunchbox » Wed Aug 06, 2014 6:23 pm I like to think that Anime Shinji didn't know squat about Seele's plans. I mean, he was aware of his mom in the Eva by the end, but everything else? He was probably pretty oblivious.

[]:             Quote Post by Atropos » Wed Aug 06, 2014 9:46 pm Misato does tell him what Seele's planning in EoE, but that's close enough to the end that he has no real capacity to stop it. On the other hand, Seele knew nothing about HIM, either, which is why he's useful to Gendo.

[]:             Quote Post by NIkkolas » Wed Aug 06, 2014 10:39 pm So wait is it SEELE or Seele? I thought it was supposed to all be in caps? Kinda not really important but figured I'd might as well ask here.

[]:             Quote Post by Nuclear Lunchbox » Wed Aug 06, 2014 10:41 pm We typically post it as Seele. Same goes for Nerv, Wille, etc.

[]:             Quote Post by pwhodges » Thu Aug 07, 2014 1:24 am They're not acronyms, so they don't need to be caps.

[]:             Quote Post by FreakyFilmFan4ever » Thu Aug 07, 2014 5:14 am [s]Just about everything[/s] Everything in The End of Evangelion came as a complete surprise to Shinji. Heck, he even thought that he would be able to come out of the cage with Eva Unit 01 save Asuka, with his unit operating just like it normally would. (Maya was never able to utter Asuka’s fate over the PA system, which is why her death and Unit 02’s defeat was a shock to him.) But instead of a typical fight scene (though probably slightly more bad-ass than usual; this is a movie after all) Shinji gets hoisted into the air by these benign Eva things and a giant Rei rises up to look into his soul. He didn’t even know that Lilith or Adam were still around and what they looked like (though Misato did mention their existence to him in EoE) or what Rei basically was outside of “replaceable human clone.” And not only do the Eva Pilots not utter “Seele,” I don’t even think the organization is mentioned to them at all. Shinji probably knew more about Jet Alone than he did about Seele.

[]:             Quote Post by Tumbling Down » Thu Aug 07, 2014 3:31 pm I've only seen End of Evangelion once, but the one of my favorite scenes is the one where Misato dumps exposition on Shinji that he clearly doesn't care about. It reminded me of the South Park episode "Towelie".

[]:             Quote Post by Kendrix » Thu Aug 07, 2014 6:01 pm Nuclear Lunchbox wrote: I like to think that Anime Shinji didn't know squat about Seele's plans. I mean, he was aware of his mom in the Eva by the end, but everything else? He was probably pretty oblivious.
